_source: Transcribed from Blood & Grit Player's Book v2.14, Ch. III–IV, VIII–X, XIII–XIV. Regenerate when those chapters change (see CLAUDE.md).

origins.name: The Drummer
origins.line: A travelling seller of tonics, Bibles, futures, or lies.
origins.boon: Once per scene reroll a failed Persuade or Deceive; in any town of size, talk your way to a bed, a meal, or credit on your word alone.
origins.burden: Once a lie of yours is found out, those who caught it take −2 to trust you for the rest of the affair, and word travels ahead of you.

origins.name: The Fallen Gentry
origins.line: Once there was a name, a house, an inheritance — and then it was gone.
origins.boon: +2 on social checks among the wealthy, the lettered, and the powerful; you read rank and the lie beneath a fine coat at a glance.
origins.burden: −1 on Fortitude saves against plain hardships — hunger, exposure, hard labor.

origins.name: The Freed
origins.line: Born or sold into bondage, you took your own liberty and walked west.
origins.boon: +2 to read a stranger's true intent and on Will saves against being cowed; +2 to slip a bond or confinement — never quite cornered while any way out remains.
origins.burden: −1 on social checks with lawmen, soldiers, and officials, who feel your wariness and return it.

origins.name: The Gambler
origins.line: A living made, and lost, at the green-felt tables of a hundred towns.
origins.boon: Permanent +2 on Sense Motive at any table; once per session reroll a single d20 and keep the better result.
origins.burden: When coin, cards, or a sure thing appear, the Keeper may call for a Will save to walk away — and old debts ride into town behind you.

origins.name: The Homesteader
origins.line: You broke sod that did not want breaking and buried more than one child under it.
origins.boon: A full night's rest in a camp you have made restores you and each companion an extra 1d6 Blood; your dogs and stock give warning before trouble arrives.
origins.burden: Away from home or kin, −1 on Initiative in the first round of any fight.

origins.name: The Laborer
origins.line: You laid rail, dug shafts, or drove spikes for wages that vanished at the company store.
origins.boon: +2 on Fortitude saves against fatigue, strain, and the wearing cold; you can labor or march long past where softer men fold.
origins.burden: −1 on checks of fine dexterity — sleight, lockwork, fuses, the surgeon's delicate art.

origins.name: The Outlaw
origins.line: There is paper on you in at least one territory.
origins.boon: Once per session declare you have been somewhere before and know one useful, unsavory fact about it; +2 with criminals and fences, and +2 to make a quick exit when the law takes an interest.
origins.burden: Lawmen and bounty men take note of you; a warrant, a posse, or an old grudge may trail you into town.

origins.name: The Scout
origins.line: You read the land like scripture — guide, hunter, wagon-pilot.
origins.boon: Ignore difficult ground afoot and never be lost beneath an open sky; +2 on Notice against ambush, tracks, and lying-in-wait out of doors — never surprised under open sky.
origins.burden: Indoors and in close crowds you take −1 on Notice.

origins.name: The Veteran
gear: Service carbine (1d10, Repeating — from your hitch)
origins.line: You served — blue or gray — and some of it wakes you in the small hours.
origins.boon: Once per session steady the line — you and each ally in earshot shrug off fear for a round; once per scene reroll a failed Reflex save to take cover.
origins.burden: First time each session sudden gunfire or the uncanny finds you, make a Dread Check or be Shaken a round.

origins.name: Came Back Wrong
origins.line: You died — and then you did not stay dead. You remember the dark on the other side.
origins.boon: Once per session, when you would drop to 0 Blood, stay standing at 1 until the end of the round; you need neither food nor sleep.
origins.burden: The Old Dark notices you first, and the living sense something wrong (−1 on first impressions with the breathing). You begin at Mark 1.

edges.name: Quick Draw
edges.desc: Draw and Strike in one motion; +2 to the roll deciding who shoots first.

edges.name: Cylinder & Sky
edges.desc: Once per round, when you reduce a foe to 0 Blood, immediately spend an unused Beat to reload.

edges.name: Dead Eye
edges.desc: Once per scene, declare a Strike a hit short of the impossible: it cannot fail, and rolls damage twice keeping the higher.

edges.name: Fan the Hammer
edges.desc: With a single-action revolver, two Beats make up to three Strikes at half the usual Multiple Attack Penalty (−2/−4).

edges.name: Gunfighter's Calm
edges.desc: Once per scene, ignore the Multiple Attack Penalty on a single Strike.

edges.name: Practiced Reload
edges.desc: Reduce the Beats to reload a favored weapon by 1 (min 0); clear your own misfires as a single Interact.

edges.name: Steady Shot
edges.desc: If you do not move, ignore the first range increment penalty and gain +1 to hit; ignore Kickback recoil.

edges.name: Throw the Stick
edges.desc: If you carry powder, hurl a lit charge in place of a Strike.

edges.name: Two-Gun
edges.desc: A pistol in each hand; your off-hand Strike does not increase your Multiple Attack Penalty for the round.

edges.name: Fleet
edges.desc: Your Speed afoot increases by 10 feet.

edges.name: Hard to Kill
edges.desc: Once per session, when you would drop to 0 Blood, drop to 1 instead and stay on your feet.

edges.name: Iron Gut
edges.desc: +2 on Fortitude saves against poison, disease, drink, and spoiled provisions.

edges.name: Saddle-Born
edges.desc: Never fall from the saddle by mishap; fight mounted without penalty.

edges.name: Tough as Rawhide
edges.desc: Gain +1 Blood per level, now and as you advance.

edges.name: Born Lucky
edges.desc: Once per session, reroll any single roll you just made and keep the kinder result.

edges.name: Cold Read
edges.desc: +3 on Insight; spend a moment to learn one true thing a person is trying to hide.

edges.name: Gallows Humor
edges.desc: Once per scene, crack wise in the face of the awful and recover 1d6 Nerve — yours, or a companion's who laughs.

edges.name: Iron Will
edges.desc: Once per scene, reroll a failed save against fear, charm, or compulsion.

edges.name: Stone Nerve
edges.desc: +2 maximum Nerve per level and +1 on Dread Checks.

edges.name: Unshakable
edges.desc: The first time each scene you would lose Nerve, lose none instead.

edges.name: Dead Shot Provider
edges.desc: Never want for food or fresh horses where game or ranches exist; your camp is always well-sited.

edges.name: Frontier Medicine
edges.desc: Your field care heals an extra 1d6; improvise a remedy from what the land offers.

edges.name: Pathfinder
edges.desc: Never truly lost; +2 on overland travel; always find water, forage, and safe ground.

edges.name: Powder Sense
edges.desc: +2 to set, spot, or disarm charges and traps; your blasts never catch a friend you can plainly see.

edges.name: Tracker
edges.desc: +3 on Survival to track; track across stone, shallow water, and the spoor of unnatural things.

edges.name: Hedge Magic
edges.desc: Learn a single Sign and gain a small Nerve pool to fuel it, though you are not a Hexer.

edges.name: Salt-Wise
edges.desc: +2 on Lore (Occult); the Old Rites take you half the usual time and materials.

edges.name: Touched
edges.desc: Gain Witch-Sight, +1 Mark, and an unsettling certainty about where the dark is thickest.

edges.name: Warded
edges.desc: Scribe a ward at a threshold the uncanny cannot cross uninvited for a night.

callingEdges.name: Run It Down
callingEdges.desc: After a Bushwhack lands, immediately Step and Stride free; your quarry must spend an action to break away.

callingEdges.name: Long Gone
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, when no enemy is looking your way, slip from sight even while observed and take a free Stride into cover.

callingEdges.name: Double or Nothing
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, before you roll, name the stake: success is one degree better; failure one degree worse.

callingEdges.name: Pistolero's Tally
callingEdges.desc: When you drop a foe with a Strike, your next Strike this round ignores the Multiple Attack Penalty.

callingEdges.name: The Long Arm
callingEdges.desc: When you Demoralize a foe or invoke the law against it, allies in earshot gain +1 on their next Strike against it.

callingEdges.name: One Shot, One Kill
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, a steadied shot against an unaware foe deals maximum Dead Aim dice and ignores hide or armor DR.

callingEdges.name: Hang Fire
callingEdges.desc: Set a charge to blow on a trigger you name; it waits, patient as the deep.

callingEdges.name: Highball Charge
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, overpack a charge: maximum dice and double burst radius.

callingEdges.name: Laid By
callingEdges.desc: Once per session, declare you laid by a useful mundane item, cache, or contact in this place — within reason, you did.

callingEdges.name: Battlefield Surgeon
callingEdges.desc: Field care in the thick of a fight provokes no reaction and restores an extra die of Blood.

callingEdges.name: Mending Hands
callingEdges.desc: Your remedies lift one lingering affliction along with the Blood they restore.

callingEdges.name: Shepherd's Word
callingEdges.desc: Spend a Beat to grant an ally in earshot an immediate save, with your bonus, against a fear already gripping them.

callingEdges.name: Hellfire Sermon
callingEdges.desc: Your invocations against the unclean reach every foe who can hear your voice.

callingEdges.name: Spirit-Spoken
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, put one yes-or-no question to the spirits about this land, its dead, or what passed here, and be answered true.

callingEdges.name: Bane-Sharpened
callingEdges.desc: When you exploit your quarry's known weakness, deal an extra die of damage; name the kind of any uncanny thing on sight.

callingEdges.name: Devoted Unto Death
callingEdges.desc: Immune to fear of the power you serve; once per scene shrug off a wound's effect for a round.

callingEdges.name: Golden Tongue
callingEdges.desc: Work a single Deceive against an entire crowd at once.

callingEdges.name: Hard Bargain
callingEdges.desc: Once per scene, work a Sign for one less Nerve than it asks; the unpaid cost comes due later.

callingEdges.name: Bitter Brew
callingEdges.desc: Keep one extra charm or poison prepared; your brews hold potency a full day longer.

signs.name: The Stilling
signs.desc: One living creature in sight is held fast, unable to act, 1 round per two levels. Backlash: on a failed working, you are stilled instead.

signs.name: Witch-Sight
signs.desc: For a scene, see the unnatural plainly — the Marked, the haunted, the thin places. Backlash: what you see may see that you see it.

signs.name: The Crimson Word
signs.desc: A creature you can see takes 3d6 harm as its own blood turns against it. Backlash: on a natural 1, you take the damage instead.

signs.name: Hollow Step
signs.desc: Step into a shadow and out of another within sight, as far as a stone's throw. Backlash: Frightened 1 until your next turn.

signs.name: The Tally
signs.desc: Ask the dark a single question; receive a true, partial, unwelcome answer. Backlash: the answer costs a Dread Check; deep truths gain Mark.

signs.name: Salt & Iron
signs.desc: Uncanny things within ten feet must save or recoil; the Marked feel its sting. Backlash: none — the kindest Sign, and the weakest.

signs.name: Borrowed Breath
signs.desc: Heal a companion 2d8, taking half that in Blood yourself. Backlash: if they die anyway, +1 Mark.

signs.name: The Calling
signs.desc: Call something from the dark to bargain or to serve. It comes. Backlash: the most dangerous Sign in the book.

weapons.name: Knife / Bowie
weapons.traits: Concealable, Agile, +1 on a Sudden Strike

featureDescs.Bushwhack: Bringing a man in is not a fair fight, and you do not pretend otherwise. Once per turn, when you Strike a quarry who is unaware of you, who has not yet acted in the fight, or whom an ally threatens, deal the listed extra damage. The ambush is the whole of the art — a bounty man forced to trade blows in the open with a cornered killer has already made his mistake.
featureDescs.Quick Hands: Years of needing your iron — or your irons, the manacle kind — a half-second sooner than the other man. You draw, holster, reload, or snap on a cuff as part of another action rather than spending a Beat on it, and add +2 to the roll that decides who acts first. Lockpicks, knots, and a fugitive's hidden pockets answer to these hands as readily as a trigger does.
featureDescs.Road Sense: You have laid enough ambushes to know when you are walking into one. Gain +2 on Notice against traps, tails, and lying-in-wait, and you are never caught flat — you act in the first round of a fight even when surprise is called against you. The hunted set snares too.
featureDescs.The Drop: With a quarry dead to rights — your iron drawn, his not — you may Demoralize as a single Beat, and a man who yields comes along quiet, hands where you can see them. Against one who calls the bluff, your first Strike counts him as unaware. Dead or alive is his choosing; you are paid the same either way.
featureDescs.Getaway: The catch is only half done until you and your man are clear of his friends. Immediately after you Strike, take a prisoner, or break free, you may Step and then Stride for free; mounted, you may instead vault to the saddle and ride, prisoner and all.
featureDescs.Hard Ride: You and your horse have run down more men — and outrun more of their kin — than you can count. Your mounted Speed increases by 10 feet, you cross rough country a fleeing man must slow for, and once per scene you may close on a single quarry who thought he had lost you, or shake a pursuit of your own, given a moment's lead and a place to turn.
featureDescs.Dead or Alive: At 10th level your name is worth nearly as much as the paper you carry. Once per scene, a Bushwhack you land against an unready quarry is an automatic critical hit. And you are never truly cornered: declare a bolt-hole — a relay horse, a confederate, a lawman who owes you, a back trail only you know — and the Keeper will honor it, once, when all seems lost. A great many men have been promised to the rope, and you are

options.name: The Stalker
options.boon: You read a quarry's trail and habits and strike from where he never looks; your Bushwhack deals extra dice against a foe who has not yet acted. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a perfect ambush against an unready quarry is an automatic critical that may end the chase before it starts.

options.name: The Posse Boss
options.boon: You ride at the head of a hunting party, swear in hands to the chase, and may Demoralize a whole group at once. Mastery (10th): once per scene, your name alone breaks lesser fugitives who know it — they scatter, surrender, or freeze.

options.name: The Trailsman
options.boon: You handle and run down mounts with ease, and ride harder than any man you hunt. Mastery (10th): once per scene, you run a quarry to ground that no horse and no head start could have saved. 11 Blood & GritV. Worldly Callings

featureDescs.Sudden Strike: When you strike a foe who is unaware of you, or whom an ally is engaging (an Off-Guard target; see Conditions), you deal extra damage as shown — the patient violence of the ambush. The dice rise as you advance.
featureDescs.Pathfinder: You move at full speed through wilderness others must crawl through, leave a trail only the gifted can follow, and add your level to checks to track, forage, or find the way.
featureDescs.Ghost / Uncanny Step / Vanish: You learn to disappear. Ghost lets you hide even while briefly observed. Uncanny Step leaves no track on any surface. Vanish lets you drop from sight once per scene, as if the land itself closed over you — out here, perhaps it has.
featureDescs.Evasion: Your reflexes are inhuman. On a successful Reflex save against an effect that would harm an area — a blast, a cave-in, a sweep of fire — you take no harm at all rather than half. On a critical success, you also end your move in any open square adjacent to the danger.

options.name: The Ghost Trail
options.boon: Gain a bonus to Sneak and Hide, and you may Hide in cover even while observed. Mastery (10th): once per scene, become wholly unseen for a round, striking from nowhere.

options.name: The Hard Trail
options.boon: Ignore the toll of harsh country, weather, and want; +2 Fortitude against the elements. Mastery (10th): once per scene, shrug off the first wound or affliction that would lay you low.

options.name: The Hunter's Trail
options.boon: You never lose a trail and gain +2 against ambush and to read a quarry. Mastery (10th): once per scene, study a foe a moment and learn the weakness that undoes it. 12 Blood & GritV. Worldly Callings

featureDescs.Fortune's Favor: Chance leans your way, and you have learned to lean back. Keep a pool of Favor equal to your PRE modifier + half your level (minimum 1), refreshed each dawn; you also recover 1 point whenever you stake something real on a genuine risk and let the dice fly. Spend a point to reroll any one d20 you just rolled and keep the better result, to add 1d6 to a check, or to force a foe to reroll a hit or a save made against you
featureDescs.Cardsharp: You are trained in games of chance and the arts that bend them — palming, marking, dealing seconds, and reading a face for the truth it is trying to hide. Gain +2 on Deceive, sleight of hand, and Sense Motive at any table, and you know at a glance whether a game is honest. In a crowd, your hands move quicker than a witness's eye.
featureDescs.Hedge Your Bets: You rarely lose it all at once. When you fail a check, you may spend a point of Favor to treat it as a success instead — but the Keeper names the price: a complication, a debt, a watching eye, a worse spot down the road. You win the hand. The game goes on.
featureDescs.Cold Deck: Once per scene, free of any cost, force a single reroll — your own, or that of a foe whose luck has run too good. And when you spend Favor to reroll, you may instead simply take the dealt card: treat the die as though it had come up an 11, no better and no worse, for the moments when steady is worth more than lucky.
featureDescs.Ace in the Hole: You are never quite as cornered as you look. Once per scene, produce something you "had ready all along" — a hideout derringer, a palmed knife, a marked card, a bribed dealer, a second key. And when you are reduced to 0 Blood, you may spend 3 Favor to come up at 1 instead and take an immediate action. The Gambler's oldest trick is not being dead when everyone else has folded.
featureDescs.Press Your Luck: Before you roll, you may ante: wager a point of Nerve or of Blood on the outcome. Succeed, and you win big — a critical success on a hit, an extra Beat on a skill, a boon the Keeper honors. Fail, and you lose the ante and the failure cuts deeper. The bolder the bet, the richer the pot the Keeper may allow.
featureDescs.Stack the Odds: Your luck spills onto those who stand with you. Once per round, free, grant an ally who can see you +1 to a roll; and you may spend your own Favor on their behalf, letting a companion reroll as though the luck were theirs.
featureDescs.The House Always Wins: At 10th level you have made chance a silent partner. Your Favor refreshes at the start of every scene rather than each dawn. And once per session you may call the turn: declare, before it is cast, that a single die — yours, or one thrown against you — comes up its best or its worst, a natural 20 or a natural 1, as though fate had dealt it from the bottom of the deck for you. The bill for such certainty always comes d

options.name: The Charmer
options.boon: Spend Favor on Deceive, Persuade, and reading a room, and gain a bonus at any table. Mastery (10th): once per scene, bend a crowd or a single mark wholly to your play.

options.name: The Duelist
options.boon: Spend Favor to add luck to your Strikes and to turn a blow aside. Mastery (10th): once per scene in a duel, turn a foe's hit into a miss, or your miss into a killing crit.

options.name: The Mechanic
options.boon: Your Favor pool grows, and once per scene you may set a die to any face you please. Mastery (10th): once per scene, stack fate entirely — take the better result on every roll for a round. 13 V. Worldly CallingsBlood & Grit

featureDescs.Deadly Aim: You may take a –2 penalty on a firearm attack to add +4 to its damage. The penalty and bonus both increase at 5th level (–3 / +6) and 9th (–4 / +8).
featureDescs.Gunhand's Edge: You gain a pool of Edges — sharp little tricks. Choose one combat Edge from Chapter IX at 1st level and at every odd level after. These are in addition to the Edges every character earns.
featureDescs.Trick Shot: Once per round, before rolling, you may declare a called shot — to disarm, to wound a limb, to shatter a lantern or a rope. Take –4 to the attack; on a hit, the Keeper grants the effect in place of, or alongside, reduced damage. Under the Iron Code (Chapter XI), a critical hit on a called shot grants the effect and full damage.
featureDescs.Lightning Hand: Your reflexes outrun thought. Drawing a holstered weapon costs no action (a free Interact), and you act before anyone you have not yet met in a fight unless they, too, have this knack. Once per fight you may take an extra Strike as a reaction the instant initiative is rolled.
featureDescs.No Quarter: When you drop a foe to 0 Blood or below with a firearm, you may immediately make one more attack against a different target within range, free of the Multiple Attack Penalty. There is no limit to how many times this may chain in a single round but your own luck. "A pistol is an argument that ends the conversation. Most arguments out here were never worth starting, which is the one thing a fast man learns too late." —

options.name: The Fanning Hand
options.boon: With a single-action revolver you may fan a second Strike each round at the Multiple Attack Penalty without spending an extra Beat. Mastery (10th): once each round, dropping a foe lets you immediately Strike another in range at no penalty.

options.name: The Fast Draw
options.boon: You always act first in a one-on-one draw, and your first Strike of a fight gains +1. Mastery (10th): the first shot you fire in a scene is a critical hit if it lands.

options.name: The Long Rifle
options.boon: Ignore the penalty at the first range increment, and Aim as a single Beat. Mastery (10th): a braced, aimed shot ignores cover and concealment and is an automatic critical against a foe who has not yet acted. 14 Blood & GritV. Worldly Callings

featureDescs.Command: As an action, bark an order. One ally within earshot may immediately take a single action — a shot, a move, a check — out of turn, gaining your PRE modifier as a bonus. Usable a number of times per scene equal to your PRE modifier (minimum 1).
featureDescs.Reputation: Your name carries. Add your level to checks to rally, deputize, requisition, or overawe, and choose at 1st level whether folk mostly fear you or trust you. Either opens doors; each closes others.
featureDescs.Hold the Line / Unflinching: Allies within sight of you gain +1 on saves against fear and the uncanny, rising to +2 at 5th level, when you yourself become immune to being Frightened. Courage, it turns out, is contagious — the only blessing in this book that is.
featureDescs.Last Stand: Once per session, when an ally within sight would drop to 0 Blood, you may shout them upright: they remain conscious and acting until the end of the fight or until struck again. Some Marshals have held a doorway with five dead men still standing at their word. "I never asked to be followed. I only stood where the road forked and would not move, and after a while there were people standing behind me. That is the whole

options.name: The Lawman
options.boon: Your commands carry weight — a bonus to Demoralize and to rally — and you may deputize allies for a scene. Mastery (10th): once per scene, your word stays a hand: a foe must lay down arms or stand frozen a round.

options.name: The Manhunter
options.boon: Name a quarry; gain bonuses to track, read, and strike them. Mastery (10th): your mark cannot shake you, and once per scene you land a decisive, fight-ending blow or capture against them.

options.name: The Shield
options.boon: You may intercept a blow meant for an ally at your side. Mastery (10th): once per scene, become the only target the enemy may strike, or turn aside a killing blow aimed at another. 15 V. Worldly CallingsBlood & Grit

featureDescs.Hawken Rifle: The heavy plains rifle is your right arm. With a two-handed rifle you ignore the penalty at the first range increment, and the first reload each round costs no extra Beat. You keep your piece clean, charged, and to hand, and you do not miss the shots a lesser hand calls lucky.
featureDescs.Dead Aim: Spend a Beat to steady your rifle — this is the Aim action — and your next shot this turn deals the extra damage shown on a hit: 1d6 at 1st level, 2d6 at 5th, and 3d6 once you are King of the High Country. Patience, a good rest, and a held breath are worth more than any luck.
featureDescs.Hard Country: The wilderness is your parlor. You are never lost in wild country, you ignore natural difficult terrain — scree, deadfall, brush, snow — and a day's hunting or foraging feeds you and up to four others. Cold, altitude, thirst, and hunger that would break other men, you simply endure.
featureDescs.Read Sign: Gain +2 to Survival and Notice to track and to read what passed — how many, how long ago, and whether it walked on two legs or something else. You leave little trail of your own: the DC to track you rises by 2, and you know when you are being followed.
featureDescs.Skinner: Spend a Beat to study a beast or an unnatural thing (Survival or Lore: Occult) and learn one weakness or trait the Keeper will honor. Against animals and the wild things of the dark you deal +1 damage, and you may harvest a kill — hide, fat, claw, tooth, gland — for coin, for trade, or for the makings of a remedy or a ward.
featureDescs.Iron Constitution: The mountains have been trying to kill you for years and have not managed it. Gain a bonus against cold, heat, thirst, hunger, poison, and disease, and once per scene you may reroll a failed Fortitude save. You heal a little faster than you have any right to.
featureDescs.Mountain Sense: You cannot be caught flat in the wild. You are never surprised out of doors, you act first in the opening round of any ambush you spring or spot, and predators give you a wide berth — they know a bigger one when they smell it.
featureDescs.Unbroken: You have seen what the high country hides in the deep snow, and naming it did not break you. Gain a bonus on Dread Checks in the wilderness, and you may spend a Beat to steady a companion who can see you, lending them your nerve to face what is coming down out of the hills.
featureDescs.King of the High Country: At 10th level the wilderness is yours, and everything in it knows your step. Your Dead Aim rises to 3d6; while out of doors you gain a standing +1 to Defense and to all saves; and once per scene a single steadied rifle shot is an automatic critical against a living or unnatural quarry you have studied with Skinner. The mountain does not give up its king easily, and neither, in the end, do you. "Folks down in the sett

options.name: The Hermit
options.boon: You have gone native in the deep country and made a peace of sorts with what lives there: beasts will not harm you unbidden, you find shelter and forage anywhere, and you read the land's moods. Mastery (10th): once per scene, call on the wild — a beast comes to your aid, a sudden storm covers your retreat, or the country itself turns a pursuer aside.

options.name: The Hunter
options.boon: Name a quarry as the Marshal marks a man, but for the beasts and monsters of the wild: gain bonuses to track, read, and damage it. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a Dead Aim shot against your quarry strikes a killing blow that fells even a great beast.

options.name: The Trapper
options.boon: You set deadfalls, snares, and pit-traps swiftly and well; a trap on ground you have readied forces the unwary to save or be caught — Grabbed, Slowed, or wounded as the trap allows. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a sprung trap is ruinous — the quarry, beast or man, is caught fast and badly hurt. 16 Blood & GritV. Worldly Callings

featureDescs.Pay Dirt: Luck is a muscle, and yours is overdeveloped. You have a pool of Pay Dirt equal to your WIT modifier + half your level (minimum 1), refreshed each dawn. Spend a point to add 1d6 to any check tied to searching, surviving, digging, or scrounging — or to declare a small fortunate find: a length of fuse, a sound timber for cover, a trickle of water, a glint of something useful in the dirt. The Keeper says how much it amo
featureDescs.Powderman: You handle blasting powder, dynamite, and raw nitro as easily as another handles a fork. Each dawn you prepare a number of field charges equal to your WIT modifier + half your level (minimum 2) — sticks, bottles, and the specialty devices you have learned to build. A stick of dynamite deals 2d6 fire in a 10-foot burst (basic Reflex, DC 10 + half your level + WIT), rising to 3d6 at 4th level, 4d6 at 7th, and 5d6 at 10
featureDescs.Grubstake: You are never quite empty-handed. You always carry working prospecting and mining tools, and once per session you may produce one mundane but useful item you "had all along" — a spare pick, a coil of wire, a candle-stub, a creased claim-map of the very ground you stand on. You also know the outfitters; in any town of size you may secure a fair price and a line of credit no questions asked, so long as your luck holds.
featureDescs.Dowse: With a forked rod, a pan, or a pendulum and a minute's concentration, make a Survival check to sense the nearest water, ore, or worked metal within a quarter-mile, and its rough direction and depth. The same gift, pushed, finds darker things — buried bodies, salted graves, and the bad veins where the Old Dark runs close to the surface. The rod always tells the truth. It simply does not always tell you what you hoped
featureDescs.Field Inventions: You are forever modifying your kit by lamplight. Choose two Devices from the list below at 4th level, and one more at 6th and at 8th; you may rebuild your loadout each dawn. Unless noted, readying a Device spends one prepared charge.
featureDescs.A Powderman’s Devices: Concussion Charge. A muffled blast of nonlethal force that deafens; gentle on walls, hard on men. Flash Powder. A blinding crack in a 10-foot burst; Fortitude or be dazzled a round, blinded on a critical failure. Grapnel & Line. A launched hook and rope to scale a height, swing a gap, or drag an object — or a man — off his feet. Repeating Fuse. Throw or place a charge as a single Beat rather than two, and add +2 to l
featureDescs.Assayer's Eye: You appraise the worth and the honesty of a thing at a glance: the value of goods and gems, the salted claim, the counterfeit coin, the true content of a vein. You instantly recognize silver and cold iron by their gleam and heft — knowledge worth more than money against the things that fear them.
featureDescs.Demolitionist: Your understanding of powder has passed from craft into art. Your bursts gain +5 feet of radius, and against structures, wagons, and the unliving your charges deal double dice. Once per scene you may daisy-chain several prepared charges into a single catastrophic blast, combining their dice and their burst — the kind of thing that reshapes a canyon wall, or the course of a battle.
featureDescs.Sapper: You dig, tunnel, and undermine at twice the speed of honest men. In a fight, spend two Beats and a point of Pay Dirt to throw up hard cover from the ground itself — a trench, a rubble-pile, a collapsed timber — or, with a charge set, to bring a wall, a bridge, or a mine-roof down on whatever stands beneath it.
featureDescs.Overcharge: Once per scene, as a single Beat, you overload a Device or your own iron: until the end of your next turn your charges and gunshots deal +2 dice and ignore the first step of a target’s resistance, and one Device fires without spending a charge. Push your luck and overcharge a second time in the same scene, and you risk a backfire — a flat check, or the thing goes off early, and on you.
featureDescs.The Mother Lode: Once per session you strike it rich. Either refill your Pay Dirt pool entirely and take a genuine windfall the Keeper honors — a fat vein, a lost cache, a buried strongbox — or, in a tight place, declare that everything goes your way for one round: treat every check you make that round as one degree of success better. The deep digging has changed you, too: you feel it in your fillings when the Old Dark stirs undergro

options.name: The Deep Claim
options.boon: You see in the dark underground and sense ore, water, and the Old Dark near at hand. Mastery (10th): once per scene the deep answers — unearth a useful relic, or turn the Old Dark's notice aside for a round.

options.name: The Lucky Claim
options.boon: Your Pay Dirt pool grows, and once per scene you may reroll a search, survival, or save for free. Mastery (10th): once per scene, declare the dice fall your way — treat any one roll as a natural 20.

options.name: The Powder Claim
options.boon: Prepare extra charges, and your bursts gain +5 feet of radius. Mastery (10th): once per scene, set off a single ruinous blast that can level a building, or a monster. 17 V. Worldly CallingsBlood & Grit

featureDescs.Field Surgery: With a kit and a few minutes, you restore 1d8 + your level in Blood to a patient, once per wound. Without a kit you may still stop bleeding and stabilize the dying on a Medicine check.
featureDescs.Anatomist / Precise Strike: You know exactly where things come apart. Against any living foe whose anatomy you understand, you may add the listed extra damage with a precise attack — a scalpel, a derringer pressed to the ribs, a well-placed blade.
featureDescs.Tonics: You brew and carry remedies: stimulants, sedatives, laudanum, antitoxins. Each dawn you may prepare a number of doses equal to your WIT modifier + half your level. Their use, and abuse, is detailed in Chapter X.
featureDescs.Alienist: You can doctor the mind as well as the body. By talking a sufferer through their terror for ten quiet minutes, you restore Nerve equal to 1d6 + your WIT modifier — reason held up like a lamp against the dark. It does not work twice on the same fright.
featureDescs.Miracle Worker: At 10th level your hands have become something the country whispers about. Once per session you may attempt the impossible at the operating table: a Beyond (DC 30) Medicine check, taken over an hour of desperate work, can pull a character back from death itself so long as the body is whole and no more than a minute has passed — or can lift a single Lasting Injury or bodily Affliction entirely. Whether the patient is

options.name: The Chemist
options.boon: Prepare extra tonics each day and name any poison or drug at a glance. Mastery (10th): brew a signature serum that grants a mighty boon for a scene, or lays a foe insensible.

options.name: The Field Medic
options.boon: Tend wounds in the thick of a fight without provoking, and reach the downed faster. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a barked field-treatment heals every ally who can hear you.

options.name: The Surgeon
options.boon: Your Treat Wounds restores an extra die, and you stabilize the dying as a single Beat. Mastery (10th): once per scene, drag a freshly-fallen ally back to their feet and fighting. 18 Blood & GritVI. Callings of Faith

featureDescs.Healing Hands: You possess the deepest healing well of any Calling: a pool of Vital Breath equal to your RES modifier + your full level (not half), refreshed each dawn. Every gift below draws on it. Where another healer counts pennies, you carry a purse.
featureDescs.Herb-Lore: You gather and prepare remedies from the living country — poultices, teas, tinctures, splints. Gain +2 on Medicine and Survival, and treat your prepared remedies as a Sawbones' Tonics. Between fights, your herb-work stabilizes the dying and tends the hurt without spending Vital Breath, so that your well is full when the shooting starts.
featureDescs.Mend the Body: By touch, spend 1 Vital Breath to heal 2d8 Blood — more per point than any other Calling can manage — or to instantly stabilize a dying soul and bring them back to their senses. Pour in more points to mend more — each further point of Vital Breath heals another 2d8.
featureDescs.Draw Out the Sickness: Spend Vital Breath to grant an immediate save against a poison, disease, or Affliction at +4 — or, for 3 Vital Breath, end one outright. You may also ease the penalty of a Lasting Injury for a day, buying a wounded companion the time the country would otherwise deny them.
featureDescs.Call Back the Breath: Within a minute of a death, you may spend half your maximum Vital Breath (rounded up) and a long, exhausting effort to call a soul back into a body still whole enough to hold it. It is never certain and never cheap, and the country sometimes takes notice when you cheat it of a meal. But you can do the one thing nearly nothing else in this book can: undo a death.
featureDescs.Soothe the Spirit: Your gift mends more than meat. Restore Nerve as readily as Blood, lift fear from the frightened, and — uniquely — suppress the symptoms of the Mark for a scene, granting a Hexer, a Dark Cultist, or any haunted soul a few hours of true peace. You cannot lift the Mark itself. But you can give the Marked a night's sleep, which they will remember as a kindness long after they have forgotten your name.
featureDescs.Hands of Life: Once per session, a great mercy pours out of you to every soul you can reach: all your companions restored, afflictions lifted, the freshly dying raised, fear washed clean away. For one moment you are the thing this country refuses to be. The cost is borne in your own body — you take on a measure of what you healed, a share of the wounds and the dread — and you cannot do it again until you have rested and grieved wha
featureDescs.A Note on the Name: "Medicine Man" carries real and specific meaning in Native American traditions. This Calling is not a portrait of any nation's sacred healer or ceremony — those, as Chapters IV and VI insist, stay off the character sheet. It is a deliberately fictional, syncretic frontier healer, blending the herb-doctor, the granny-woman, the faith-healer, and the bonesetter of many trail traditions. Tables are warmly encouraged to

options.name: The Dreaming Way
options.boon: You walk in visions and read omens for guidance. Mastery (10th): once per scene, walk the dream to foresee a danger, or to find what is hidden or lost.

options.name: The Mending Way
options.boon: Your remedies are potent and may lift a lingering affliction. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a healing rite mends every ally near you.

options.name: The Warding Way
options.boon: Craft charms that turn aside the dark and its lesser servants. Mastery (10th): once per scene, draw a circle of protection no uncanny thing may enter. 20 Blood & GritVI. Callings of Faith

featureDescs.Sacraments: You have a pool of Grace equal to your PRE modifier + half your level (minimum 1), refreshed at the dawn Mass (or its private equivalent). Grace fuels every rite below. A Padre who has missed his offices too long begins the day with the pool halved — the Church is exacting about its bookkeeping.
featureDescs.Rite of Exorcism: Your signature work. Against a possessing spirit, a controlling influence, or the Mark working in a living soul, spend Grace and intone the Rite — a contest of your Sacrament DC (10 + half level + PRE) against the thing's Will, repeated each round you persist. Win the contest and you suppress it for the scene; win it twice running and you cast it out entirely. It is slow, it is loud, and it makes you the only thing i
featureDescs.Holy Water & Relic: You may bless water by the vial; a thrown vial deals 1d6 holy damage (Scatter 5 ft) to the uncanny and burns the Marked. You also carry a true relic — a saint's bone, a splinter, a medal worn smooth — which grants one reroll each day on a save against the unnatural, and which the restless dead are loath to approach.
featureDescs.Sanctuary: Spend Grace and a minute to consecrate a space. Uncanny things must make a Will save against your Sacrament DC to enter or to strike those within; allies inside recover Nerve each round and gain +2 on Will saves. The ground holds until dawn, until deconsecrated, or until you are carried out of it.
featureDescs.Viaticum: The last rites are also the kindest medicine. Spend Grace to heal 2d8 Blood and halt the dying by touch. Should the patient die regardless, you shrive them: their soul departs in peace, beyond the dark's reach — they cannot rise as restless dead — and the quiet of a good death restores you 1d6 Nerve.
featureDescs.The Greater Rite: You learn one major liturgical working: Bell, Book, and Candle (a ten-minute rite that banishes a lesser uncanny outright, or a greater one for a day and a night), or the Consecration of a Haunt (which lays a binding on the very stones of a haunted place). Choose at 7th; the Keeper may permit more in play.
featureDescs.The Church Militant: Once per session, draw on the whole weight of the Church behind you. As an action, name the unclean in Latin and command it: it makes a Will save against your Sacrament DC or is destroyed if a lesser thing, banished if a greater, and in any case driven Frightened and Slowed. Every ally who can hear the Rite is immune to fear until the scene ends. Two thousand years of the dead speak the words with you, and for one mo

options.name: The Exorcist
options.boon: Rebuke the uncanny and loosen its grip upon the possessed. Mastery (10th): once per scene, cast out a possessing or lesser horror entirely.

options.name: The Martyr
options.boon: Take a wound meant for another, and endure where others fall. Mastery (10th): once per scene, interpose against a killing blow, or fall and rise once more, unbroken.

options.name: The Shepherd
options.boon: Bless allies who can hear you and ward them against fear. Mastery (10th): once per scene, raise a sanctuary that shields all nearby from harm for a round. 21 VI. Callings of FaithBlood & Grit

featureDescs.Conviction: You have a pool of Conviction equal to your PRE modifier + half your level (minimum 1), refreshed each dawn. Conviction fuels your sermons and miracles. It is faith made countable — and it runs dry exactly when you need it most.
featureDescs.Sermon: Spend 1 Conviction and speak for a round. Allies who hear you regain Nerve equal to your PRE modifier and gain +1 on Will saves until the scene ends. The unfaithful may mock; they steady all the same.
featureDescs.Ward: Spend 1 Conviction to inscribe or invoke a ward against the uncanny. Marked and unnatural things must make a Will save to cross it or strike those within. A ward holds until dawn or until you fall.
featureDescs.Brimstone: Your invective becomes a smiting. As an attack, point and condemn a single creature within sight; spend 1 Conviction to deal the listed holy damage (doubled against the Marked and the uncanny), Will save for half. It is loud, and it draws notice.
featureDescs.Lay On Hands: By touch and prayer, spend Conviction to heal 1d8 Blood per point spent, or to halt bleeding and stabilize the dying. The same gift, turned on the unnatural, deals that much instead.
featureDescs.Revelation: At 10th level, faith burns through the veil. Once per session, as an action, you may spend 3 Conviction to lay the truth of a place or a creature bare before all who can hear you: the uncanny within sight is named aloud — its nature, its hunger, and the one thing it fears — and is struck Frightened 1 for the scene, while your companions ignore the next Dread Check they would face. The Word, spoken once with perfect c

options.name: The Hellfire Testament
options.boon: Your Brimstone strikes an additional foe within reach of the first. Mastery (10th): once per scene, call down a pillar of fire upon the unclean.

options.name: The Revival Testament
options.boon: Your sermons mend the faithful and steady their nerve. Mastery (10th): once per scene, a great revival heals and emboldens every ally who can hear you, and lifts the freshly fallen.

options.name: The Wrathful Testament
options.boon: Gain a bonus against the uncanny and those who serve it. Mastery (10th): once per scene, your smite banishes a lesser horror outright. 22 Blood & GritVI. Callings of Faith

featureDescs.Spirit-Sight: You perceive the spirits of the living world and the unquiet dead, the thin places, and the moods of the country itself. You may address a spirit of place and, with courtesy, be answered — though the spirits keep their own counsel and their own grudges.
featureDescs.The Helping Spirits: One or more spirits walk with you — choose an Aspect, such as the Wolf (the hunt and the pack), the Raven (cunning and the dead), the River (mending and endurance), or the Elder (memory and counsel). The Aspect grants a standing boon and a called-upon boon fueled by your pool of Breath (RES modifier + half level, refreshed each dawn). You may make peace with a second Aspect at 6th level.
featureDescs.Mending Hands: Spend Breath to heal 1d8 Blood per point by touch and song, or to ease a frightened soul — granting back Nerve or a fresh save against an Affliction. The spirits mend what they are asked to, in their own time and to their own ends.
featureDescs.Spirit-Walk: Send your spirit out from your body to scout unseen, or step a short way through the spirit-world to cross ground you could not otherwise reach. Your body lies senseless while you wander, and the longer you stay out, the harder the road back; tarry too long and you risk a Dread Check to find your way home.
featureDescs.Don the Aspect: For a scene, take on the gifts of a Helping Spirit: the Wolf's claws and speed, the Raven's wings and sight, the River's tirelessness, the Elder's uncanny insight. The change is partial and temporary, and it asks a little of you each time — the spirits lend, they do not give.
featureDescs.Commune: Sit with the land, the weather, or the recently dead and learn what they know: the way through, the coming storm, the game and the water, the name of what walks at night. With proper respect you may also lay a restless spirit to rest, which is the truest service a Shaman renders, and the one the dead remember.
featureDescs.Become the Mask: Once per session you may give yourself wholly over to a great spirit for a scene, embodying its full power — a storm, a beast-king, an ancestor of terrible weight. While the spirit rides, you wield gifts far beyond a mortal's. But when it departs, make a Will save (DC the Keeper sets) or a little of it stays, and a little of you does not return. Wear the mask too often and the question stops being whether it comes of

options.name: The Ancestor Pact
options.boon: The honored dead counsel you; gain rerolls on lore and warning of danger. Mastery (10th): once per scene, call an ancestral host to stand and fight beside you a round.

options.name: The Beast Pact
options.boon: A spirit-beast walks with you, lending its senses and its aid. Mastery (10th): once per scene, take on the great beast's shape, or call it forth in full.

options.name: The Land Pact
options.boon: Command small workings of wind, water, ember, and stone. Mastery (10th): once per scene, loose a great elemental working — a sudden storm, a swallowing earth. 23 VI. Callings of FaithBlood & Grit

choice.label: Aspect
options: the Wolf (the hunt and the pack)

featureDescs.The Hunt: As a single Beat, name one creature you can see as your quarry. Against that quarry you gain +1 to hit and to all checks to find, track, and study it; you ignore any supernatural concealment it enjoys; and you may not be forced to flee it by fear. You may have one quarry at a time and may shift it with another Beat. The Hunt is the whole of the art: choose your prey, and never lose the trail.
featureDescs.Sanctified Iron: Each dawn, anoint your weapons with salt, silver-wash, oil, and a muttered rite. Until next dawn they count as silver, cold iron, and blessed for the purpose of overcoming the uncanny's resistances, and deal +1 damage to the Marked and the unnatural. At 6th level, all three at once; before that, choose one each morning and guess well.
featureDescs.Steeled Nerve: You have stared into too many open graves to flinch at one more. Gain +2 on all Dread Checks and saves against fear, and halve the Nerve you lose to your current quarry's kind. The horror is still there. You have simply made a profession of not looking away.
featureDescs.Recognize the Unclean: Study a creature or a person for a round and make a Lore: Occult check: learn whether they bear the Mark or are unnatural, what manner of thing they are, and one weakness the Keeper will honor — silver, fire, running water, true names, the dawn. The gift is exact, and it is the most dangerous thing you own, for it tempts you to trust it absolutely.
featureDescs.Judgment: Once per quarry, declare Judgment before a Strike. On a hit, deal the listed extra holy-and-silver damage; on a critical hit, the quarry must save (DC 10 + half level + WIT) or be Frightened and Slowed, losing a Beat. Judgment refreshes when you name a new quarry.
featureDescs.Relentless: When your quarry drops or flees, you do not relent: immediately Stride toward it for free, and your first Strike in pursuit ignores the Multiple Attack Penalty. A wounded thing that runs from a Witch Hunter is merely choosing where it dies.
featureDescs.The Pyre: At 10th level you are the bane of unclean things. Your Judgment recharges at the start of every scene, not merely with each new quarry. And once per session, when you reduce an uncanny quarry to 0 Blood, you may perform the rites of ending — salt, fire, the severed name — and ensure it stays dead: it does not rise, reform, or return. Nothing the Keeper has prepared survives the Pyre. The cost is the man you have beco

options.name: The Inquisitor
options.boon: You sense lies and the Mark upon a soul, and you resist the uncanny's influence. Mastery (10th): once per scene, speak a binding word that holds a lesser uncanny thing helpless a round.

options.name: The Slayer
options.boon: Deal extra damage against your quarry's kind. Mastery (10th): your Judgment can fell even a great horror, and a killing blow against the uncanny cannot be shrugged off.

options.name: The Warden
options.boon: Scribe wards that bar the uncanny and shield others from the Old Dark. Mastery (10th): once per scene, sanctify the ground around you, which no uncanny thing may cross. 24 Blood & GritVII. Callings of the Old Dark

featureDescs.Devotion: You have a pool of Devotion — your patron's favor — equal to your RES modifier + half your level, refreshed through observance: the kept fast, the night vigil, the rite performed at the proper hour. It fuels your Dark Communion. A Dark Cultist who lets devotion lapse finds the favor cold exactly when it is needed.
featureDescs.Dark Communion: You work Signs and Old Rites (Chapter XIII), paid in Devotion (or, when that runs dry, in Nerve and Blood), and you receive them as what they are to you: genuine gifts from the thing you serve. You begin knowing two Signs and learn another at each Sign learned.
featureDescs.The Glad Mark: You begin already touched (Mark 1), as a Hexer does — but where the Hexer is dragged along the track, you walk it, willingly, eyes open. Reckless workings advance your Mark as theirs do; the difference is that you do not mourn the steps. And unlike any other Calling, a rising Mark repays you (see Gifts of the Patron). You are becoming something else on purpose, and calling it grace.
featureDescs.Sacrifice: Devotion is best shown in blood. Spend your own Blood to empower a working at the Blood-Price rate, or offer a worthier sacrifice — a beast, a treasure, a life — to petition your patron for a boon beyond your level, which the Keeper adjudicates. Great gifts demand great offerings, and the patron always knows the difference between a sacrifice that costs you and one that does not.
featureDescs.Gifts of the Patron: As the Mark claims you, the patron remakes you in its image. At Mark 2, 3, and 4, choose a Gift: unnatural resilience (DR), a malformed weapon-limb, sight that pierces any dark, a voice that staggers the faithful, the cold strength of the deep places. Each is a true power and a true disfigurement. The more monstrous you become, the more the dark can pour through you.
featureDescs.Rapture: Once per scene, give yourself over to ecstatic communion: become immune to fear and pain (ignore Frightened, gain DR) and let your workings surge in power. While raptured you cannot retreat, and you cannot act against your patron's interest — for in that moment you have no interest of your own.
featureDescs.Vessel: Once per session you may invite your patron to descend fully into you for a scene. You become its herald upon the earth, wielding power no mortal should hold — and the Keeper plays you while it lasts, for it is no longer entirely your body. When the scene ends, make a Will save against a DC the Keeper sets. Fail, and a little more of you stays behind the veil; fail at high Mark, and you do not come back at all, but s

options.name: The Cold Deep
options.boon: Spend Devotion to drain warmth (Slowed, Drained, cold damage) and steady yourself against your end (reroll a failed Fortitude save to stop your bleeding; ignore fear). Greater (9th): once per session, open a sphere of annihilating cold that withers all within and shows each witness the indifferent end.

options.name: The Devourer
options.boon: Slaying or sacrificing heals you and grants Devotion; your Gifts favor claws and toughness; you regenerate while fed. Greater (9th): once per scene, swell into a thing of teeth — large, swift, regenerating — devouring what you bring down.

options.name: The Long Trail
options.boon: Spend Devotion to lay a withering death-touch (necrotic cold) and to sense the dying — you know who in your sight is marked to die soon, and may bid a freshly-slain foe rise and serve you a round. Your Gifts favor a deathless calm, an unerring eye for a mortal wound, and a body slow to quit. Greater (9th): once per session, pronounce a death sentence on one you can name — short of a true miracle t

options.name: The Red Sermon
options.boon: Spend Devotion to borrow a face and a honeyed voice — charm or compel those who hear you, pass for someone trusted, and feed on a crowd's devotion to refill your pool. You gather a small flock that believes, and their belief is meat. Your Gifts favor a stolen face, a compelling word, and a hunger worn as warmth. Greater (9th): once per session, hollow a gathering — every soul who can hear you is g

options.name: The Thing Beneath the Mountain
options.boon: Spend Devotion to call on the deep stone — a crushing grip, a hide of grinding rock (DR and resistance), and tremor-sense through earth and floorboard. You see in the lightless dark, never lose your way underground, and the buried answers when you knock. Your Gifts favor stone flesh, a crushing strength, and the secrets of ore and vein. Greater (9th): once per session, wake the mountain a little —

options.name: The Whisperer
options.boon: Spend Devotion to pluck a secret from a mind or the air, and to whisper madness — confusion, fear, or a Dread Check. Greater (9th): once per session, speak the Unspeakable Word — unmake a mind, or wring one true and terrible answer from your Patron. 26 Blood & GritVII. Callings of the Old Dark

featureDescs.The Conduit: Your power is stolen worship, and your pool of it is called Tribute. It equals your PRE modifier + half your level, but its maximum swells with the size and fervor of the flock you have deceived — a packed revival tent raises the ceiling high; a doubting crowd or a night alone drops it to nothing. Tribute refills when you preach the false gospel to believers. Cut off from a congregation to fleece, you are merely a li
featureDescs.False Gospel: You may work a small repertoire of Signs (Chapter XIII) — but in your hands they wear the mask of holy wonders: light where there should be shadow, a dove where there should be a worm. None but the witch-sighted perceive the dark beneath. You pay for these workings in Tribute, never your own Nerve. You begin knowing two such wonders and learn another with each Stolen Wonder feature.
featureDescs.Honeyed Word: Few resist a prophet. Gain +3 on Persuade and Deceive against crowds and the faithful, and once per scene issue a Command the devout obey as scripture (Will save to resist, at disadvantage for true believers).
featureDescs.Visit the Cost: This is the black heart of the Calling, and the thing that sets you apart from every other soul in this chapter. When a working would make you pay — a Backlash, a Blood price, an advance of the Mark — you may instead lay that cost upon a member of your flock: a believer takes the wound, the ruin, the creeping Mark, while you walk away clean and haloed. The dark does not care who bleeds, only that someone does. But ea
featureDescs.Feed the Dark: Once per session, offer the massed devotion of a gathering up to your unseen patron. Refill your Tribute and gain a surge besides — and the dark grows fat, while your flock comes away hollowed, hungrier for you, and a little less themselves than they were. They will not know why. They will only know they need another sermon.
featureDescs.The Hollow Crown: Once per session, for a single scene, you throw the doors wide and let the dark wear your gospel openly through you: tremendous power flows — great smitings, healings, terrors, commands — and the crowd will die at your asking, and some may. But you are no longer entirely the one preaching; the Keeper may put a word in your mouth and turn your hand, as the thing behind the curtain stretches into the room it has rented
featureDescs.Three Debts: Hexer, Witch, False Prophet: The Hexer borrows from the dark directly and is Marked from the first hour. The Witch inherits an older craft and pays in her own Nerve. The False Prophet does neither — they run a confidence game on the dark's behalf and arrange for the deceived to settle the account. The Hexer dreads the bill. The Witch budgets for it. The False Prophet mails it to a parishioner and passes the collection plate again. "He laid hands

options.name: The Borrowed Saint
options.boon: Healing and blessing wonders cost 1 less Tribute and run deeper; you read as genuinely holy to Witch-Sight and any test of faith. Greater (9th): once per session, stage a public miracle that binds every witness to you as devoted and floods your Tribute.

options.name: The Doomsayer
options.boon: Fear and smiting wonders surge; once per scene pronounce a small judgment — a blight, a swarm, a sickness, a curse of ill luck. Greater (9th): once per session, prophesy and unleash a scene-wide calamity, its cost paid by the deceived flock.

options.name: The Golden Calf
options.boon: You and your faithful prosper — conjure good fortune and draw a sourceless income wherever you preach. Greater (9th): once per session, seal a covenant of plenty over a town for the arc, while the dark quietly mortgages the deceived. 27 VII. Callings of the Old DarkBlood & Grit

featureDescs.Signs: You know and may work Signs — the rites of the Old Dark (Chapter XIII). You begin knowing two and learn another at each even level. Working a Sign costs Nerve, and sometimes Blood, and always risks Backlash.
featureDescs.Witch-Sight: You see what is truly there: the residue of violence, the Mark upon a soul, the thin places where the world wears through. This sight cannot be unlearned, and some nights you wish it could.
featureDescs.Marked: You begin at Mark 1 and advance the Mark faster than others — reckless use of Signs, and the knowledge they bring, pushes you along the track toward the thing you are slowly becoming. Power and ruin are the same road.
featureDescs.Blood Price: From 5th level you may pay for a Sign in Blood (Hit Points) when your Nerve runs short, at a rate of 2 Blood per point of Nerve. The Old Dark does not care which red ledger you draw from.
featureDescs.Adept: At 10th level, choose one Sign you know. You may work it without spending Nerve once per scene — though never without consequence.
featureDescs.Working a Sign — the Short of It: Declare the Sign and pay its Nerve (or Blood). If it forces a save, the target rolls against your Sign DC = 10 + half your level + your RES modifier. Backlash strikes on the listed trigger — most often a natural 1 on any roll the working requires, or a failed working. The deep truths a Sign reveals may also demand a Dread Check of their own. Knowing is never free; for a Hexer, it is merely cheaper at first.

options.name: The Conjurer
options.boon: Bind a Sign or Rite into a charm anyone may trigger without Nerve (keep WIT-mod charms); +2 and half-time on Old Rites. Greater (9th): once per session, craft a great charm that wards a whole scene — allies gain a Ward, the uncanny recoil — paid for with something dear.

options.name: The Hollow-Born
options.boon: Feel pain and fear dimly — DR 2 vs nonmagical harm, ignore the first Nerve lost each scene — but advance the Mark faster. Greater (9th): at Mark 4+, once per scene work any Sign without Nerve, paying in Blood instead — each working a step nearer the sixth Mark.

options.name: The Pact-Sworn
options.boon: Once per scene, turn a failed Sign or Will save into a success by taking a Debt; on your third Debt the Patron calls it in — a demand, and +1 Mark. Greater (9th): once per session, when you would die, the Patron stays death's hand — stabilize at 1 Blood and rise, at the cost of +1 Mark.

options.name: The Spiritist
options.boon: See and briefly question the recently dead; set a willing spirit to watch and warn (+5 Notice). Greater (9th): once per session, invite a willing spirit to act through you for a round, borrowing a skill or memory; the unwilling risks possession. 28 Blood & GritVII. Callings of the Old Dark

featureDescs.The Craft: You know and work Signs (Chapter XIII), beginning with two and learning another at each even level, paid in Nerve and risking Backlash as the Hexer's are. But the Craft is inherited, not borrowed: you do not begin Marked, and you advance the Mark only through genuine recklessness, never automatically. The dark did not hand you this. Your line did.
featureDescs.Familiar: A small beast is bound to you — a cat, a crow, a hare, a toad, a black snake. It is cannier than any animal, scouts and spies at your bidding, can deliver a touch-range Sign for you, and grants a standing boon while near (a +2 to one sense or skill befitting its nature). You share its senses at will. Should it die, you are Sickened until you can bind another over a long night's rite — and you will feel the loss far l
featureDescs.The Evil Eye: With a look and a muttered word, lay a minor bane on a creature you can see (Will save vs your Sign DC): –1 to its rolls and a run of small misfortunes for a round per two levels. It costs no Nerve, but everyone who sees you do it remembers your face.
featureDescs.Brew: Given a kitchen, a fire, and time, you brew the work of the Goods chapter and more — healing poultices, sleeping draughts, truth-loosening teas, and poisons that no assayer can name. Treat your brews as a Sawbones' Tonics, with effects you and the Keeper devise; the dose is in the making, and the making is in your hands.
featureDescs.Hex: Your maledictions grow teeth. As an action and 1 Nerve, lay a true Hex on a foe (Will save vs Sign DC): Frightened, Clumsy, Sickened, or cursed with ruinous luck (foes' crits against allies, your crits against the cursed) for a scene. A Hexed soul knows it is Hexed, and knows who did it, which is sometimes the worst of it.
featureDescs.The Old Witch: At 10th level your curses set like winter ground: a target who fails a Hex carries it until it is lifted by rite, apology, or your death. Once per session you may speak a true curse of dire and lasting effect, the kind that follows a bloodline. You have outlived everyone who feared you young. The ones who fear you now are right to.
featureDescs.Witch and Hexer — the Difference: Both work Signs and pay in Nerve, but the Hexer's power is a loan (begins Marked, races the Mark) while the Witch's is inheritance (no starting Mark, advances it only through abuse). A Hexer can become a Witch's cautionary tale; a Witch can become a Hexer the day she reaches for something her line never taught her. Keep the distinction at the table — it is the whole tragedy of the chapter.

options.name: The Cursewife
options.boon: Your Hexes and Evil Eye strike harder and cling tighter; Hex two targets at once; lifting your curse costs dearly. Greater (9th): once per session, lay a curse of escalating ruin on a wrongdoer until they amend, fall, or flee the country.

options.name: The Familiar-Bound
options.boon: Your familiar grows clever and hardy, delivers your Signs, and lets you see and hear through it at any distance. Greater (9th): swap places with it once per scene and share wounds or Blood; should you fall, it carries your spirit to a new dawn — once.

options.name: The Greenwitch
options.boon: Brews never spoil and take half the time; keep a free healing or warding brew; +2 Survival and Medicine; beasts will not harm you unbidden. Greater (9th): once per scene, call the wild — walling thorns, a healing green circle, or a trail the land hides until dawn.

options.name: The Moon-Daughter
options.boon: See in the dark; once a night take to the air until dawn; slip into a sleeper's dreams to glean or leave a secret. Greater (9th): once per session, ride the night sky to any place you have been, carrying your coven, and walk the dreams of the distant sleeping. 29 VII. Callings of the Old DarkBlood & Grit

compass.name: Lawful Neutral — the Letter of the Law

vices: drink
the cards
laudanum
violence, arrived at too easily
pride in the Word
solitude, and the flask that makes it bearable
the bargains; they keep working
a temper like dry powder
other people's secrets
the needle of the far horizon
tobacco, chewed to keep the hands busy
a grudge, fed and watered like stock
debts he means to settle, someday
the sound of his own voice, preaching or not
cheating, only ever a little
a fondness for other folks' ruin
sleep, and what waits in it
the whip hand, used more than it's needed
a promise, easy given, hard kept
the dice, loaded or not
lost: the land, to a bank with better lawyers
kin, to a fever the doctor never named
a good name, signed away on a bad night
the war took it; he stopped asking what to call it
a partner, on a trail that went quiet
the faith that used to fit like a coat
a child, and the grave is a week's ride east
a hand, to a thresher that didn't care whose it was
the ranch, to three bad seasons running
a brother, to the same coin flip that spared him
the voice for singing, one cold night on watch
a wife, to a sickness with no cure and no name
the taste for whiskey, and most everything after
a son, to a war that ended before he came home
the house, burned for the insurance or the spite, nobody ever said which
seen: lights over the dry lake that walked against the wind
a dead man at his own funeral, standing at the back
the thing the coyotes would not touch
a town empty at noon, suppers still warm
tracks that started nowhere and ended at a wall
a preacher's shadow that knelt when he did not
the well that answers, if you ask it twice
what the winter left on the pass, arranged neatly
a rider who cast two shadows, going different ways
cattle standing dead on their feet, still grazing
a photograph that develops a stranger every time
the same star fall twice in one night, same spot
a baptism where the water pulled back from the preacher's hand
footprints in the dust that walked backward out of a locked room
a wedding party, all in black, none of them speaking
the moon rise twice, an hour apart
moving: a debt that gathers interest in more than money
a name on a list, and the list is shorter now
a letter from someone who should not be writing letters
the paper on their head, and who carries it
a promise made over a grave
the thing that burned the church went west
a sister who stopped writing back
the only map to the claim, memorized and burned
a bounty that keeps climbing no matter who's collecting
a curse passed hand to hand like a bad coin
kin looking for kin who don't want finding
a Pinkerton who asks about the wrong grave
a debt owed to something that isn't a bank
a wanted poster with the wrong face and the right name
the last letter a dead man wrote, still being delivered
a claim jumper two towns back and closing
